# UC-21.9.6 · SCADA RTU Communication Health Across Remote Sites

## Description

Silent RTU loss leaves operators blind at lift stations and remote wells; age since last good poll drives prioritized truck rolls before overflows.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from SCADA RTU Communication Health Across Remote Sites to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Map protocol timeouts to `poll_ok=0`. Set `age_sec` threshold to 3× expected scan period. Exclude maintenance windows via site lookup. **Domain context:** Water RTUs often use poll/response over radio or cellular—staleness is a common failure mode before SCADA shows “last good” values. **Splunk:** `stats latest(_time)` without time window uses all data—scope with `| where _time>relative_time(now(),"-24h")` or use `last` in a time-bounded subsearch for dashboards.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub, SCADA front-end logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=water` `sourcetype="scada:rtu"` (rtu_id, site_id, poll_ok, response_ms).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map protocol timeouts to `poll_ok=0`. Set `age_sec` threshold to 3× expected scan period. Exclude maintenance windows via site lookup. **Domain context:** Water RTUs often use poll/response over radio or cellular—staleness is a common failure mode before SCADA shows “last good” values. **Splunk:** `stats latest(_time)` without time window uses all data—scope with `| where _time>relative_time(now(),"-24h")` or use `last` in a time-bounded subsearch for dashboards.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=water sourcetype="scada:rtu"
| stats latest(_time) as last_ok, latest(response_ms) as last_ms, latest(poll_ok) as last_poll by rtu_id, site_id
| eval age_sec=now()-last_ok
| where last_poll=0 OR age_sec > 900 OR last_ms > 5000
| table site_id, rtu_id, last_poll, last_ms, age_sec
```

Understanding this SPL

**SCADA RTU Communication Health Across Remote Sites** — Silent RTU loss leaves operators blind at lift stations and remote wells; age since last good poll drives prioritized truck rolls before overflows.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=water` `sourcetype="scada:rtu"` (rtu_id, site_id, poll_ok, response_ms). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub, SCADA front-end logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: water; **sourcetype**: scada:rtu. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=water, sourcetype="scada:rtu". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rtu_id, site_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where last_poll=0 OR age_sec > 900 OR last_ms > 5000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **SCADA RTU Communication Health Across Remote Sites**): table site_id, rtu_id, last_poll, last_ms, age_sec


Step 3 — Validate
Compare poll health, timestamps, and quality bits with the SCADA front-end and field tech tickets for the same RTU or site. Confirm `_time` matches the protocol scan time you expect, not just log receipt time.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (RTU × site), Table (oldest staleness), Line chart (response time trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=water sourcetype="scada:rtu"
| stats latest(_time) as last_ok, latest(response_ms) as last_ms, latest(poll_ok) as last_poll by rtu_id, site_id
| eval age_sec=now()-last_ok
| where last_poll=0 OR age_sec > 900 OR last_ms > 5000
| table site_id, rtu_id, last_poll, last_ms, age_sec
```

## Visualization

Status grid (RTU × site), Table (oldest staleness), Line chart (response time trend).
